## [00:00 - 03:04]

So in this lesson we're going to take a look at product pages and specifically how to optimise them to get the most out of all the traffic you push to those pages. Your product page is one of the most important pages on your website because for us at QuadLock it's the page that gets most of the traffic. So when we're advertising we're not pushing people necessarily to our home page or collection page. What we're doing is we're pushing people to the specific product page of the solution or the product that they've initially shown interest to. So they shouldn't have to navigate. They land on this. This page becomes the landing page. They land on this page and from this page they should be able to be convinced that yes this product is what I want and I'm going to buy it. Okay so let's jump in and take a look at one of our QuadLock product pages. Now as you can see this is where someone would land if they showed interest in purchasing our motorcycle or mirror mount kit for all iPhone devices. So from within this first load of the page they can see everything that they really need to know. So when they land they land on the video. Now our product is one of those things that you can look at the photos down here and sort of understand how it works but the better way to understand how it works we've found over the years is to actually have a video. So if they play that video you can see in the first three seconds bang that phone locks onto that mount. It's not going anywhere and then we can start telling them about how it works and what it does, the features and benefits. Now all of these features and benefits and everything that's in this video has come from the customer survey that taught us this is what customers care about. This is what they want to know. So they can watch this video, they can then look at a number of different photos showing the product and then they've got everything they need to know about here. You've got reviews, super important. We see a lot of people actually click this reviews. They come down here, they can have a look at what it is that's been reviewed. They can read the reviews, they can read one star, five star. They can get really confident with the product. They can see that they've got a buy now pay later option whether that be after pay, Cezul, whatever it is and they can actually purchase the product relatively quickly. And if they're not 100% sure on the product they can then go to a how to video and learn even more about the product which tells them if it will fit their bike or not. Now when optimizing your page you need to think what are reasons people wouldn't buy my product. And sometimes people, they may not trust the product. So money back guarantee, 30-day money back guarantee, you put that in there. Free shipping, you put this in here and you see this doesn't take you to another page, it just pops up and you're still on the product page. So they can get the information they want and then they're straight back on the product page where they buy. They can very quickly select what they want, they can see the price changing and they can

## [03:04 - 06:04]

understand that oh you can pay with Apple Pay Visa, Mastercard, Amix, PayPal, Cezul, Afterpay, whatever it is they want and they buy. You can add to cart from here quickly. They don't even need to scroll down the page to look at more information if they don't want to. But they can if they want. They can scroll down, they can see 41,000 reviews, they can see that we're active on social media, they can understand the features and benefits at further detail. And at this point most people would have enough to check out. But we still get a lot of questions and what we've done over the years is take those questions we get from social or people emailing in, they're asking us what's included, they want to know different tech specs about the product. We've tried to include all this in the page in a way that doesn't confuse the user when they're at this part of the page. Here they can just buy. But if they are that user that needs more information they can come down here, they can find drawings, technical drawings, all the information that they need and at that point if they're not sold they're probably not going to buy. A key learning we've had is that you never want to take people away from your product page so the information that they need to know whether it's money back guarantee or free shipping and how long that's going to take should all be done through pop-ups. That way they can click on it, understand it, read it, it gives you some way to put a little bit more information without cluttering your product page and then they can close it and they're straight back into the buying process. Another awesome strategy on your product page to get trust is to highlight your reviews. Now you can highlight your reviews just with the stars and the total amount of reviews but then you can also give them more information of what people have actually said about the reviews and we purposely split that out because some people just want to see that big thousand plus reviews, five stars or some people want to go click through and read those reviews so you give people both options, whatever one they want to go with. Another thing we do because we have six different websites globally is on the product page we send out our reviews per domain so that we have actually different reviews on different product page. If you have any motorcycle reviews on the dot-com page they'll be different to the motorcycle reviews on the EU page and that's really good because it comes up as unique content and helps us rank better in those individual markets. So another thing we noticed by watching Hot Jar Recordings of people actually live using our site is that a lot of people were checking out without ever reading the description so what we did is we really decluttered the first load of our page and put all the extra information below the fold because most people don't need to know it but we do have a subset of customers that really into it, they want to read the description, they want to know exactly what's included and they want maybe even some tech specs about the product so those customers should be able to get that information but in a way that doesn't detract from the product page and clutter it up and detract from the experience of the majority of the customers that don't need to know that information. So as an action item for this lesson why don't you pick two or three of these things and

see how you can apply them to your site. So do that now and I'll catch you in the next lesson.
